hero_085.earlyGame: In the early game, use Skill 1's energy sphere to track enemy heroes, gain vision, and aim for knock-ups to support your allies. Use Skill 2 to automatically attack and help with damage trades in lane. With the passive deer transformation, you can step forward without fear of hard CC.
hero_085.midGame: Attach to an allied hero with Skill 3, granting them a shield that absorbs all damage to increase their survivability. While attached, all skill cooldowns are reduced by 30%, so it's important to frequently cycle Skills 1 and 2 to secure vision and deal extra damage, preparing for teamfights.
hero_085.lateGame: In the late game, the shield's absorption amount increases based on max HP, making attaching to your allied carry extremely powerful. If you manually detach before the shield breaks, 50% of the cooldown is refunded. Utilize this mechanic to quickly and repeatedly attach, protecting your allies.
hero_085.teamfight: In teamfights, use Skill 3 to attach to your team's main damage dealer, granting them a shield, and use Skill 1 to poke the enemy. When hit by a powerful enemy hard CC, intentionally take the hit to turn into a deer via your passive, reducing the movement speed of surrounding enemies by 30%, and then use Skill 3 to flexibly provide a movement speed buff to your allies.
hero_085.commonMistakes: A common mistake is not detaching until the shield is completely destroyed. If it's destroyed, the cooldown starts fully. When focused and the shield is about to break, it's crucial to remember to manually detach to refund 50% of the cooldown.
hero_085.strengths: The greatest strength is the ability to reliably protect allies while fighting by attaching and providing a shield with Skill 3. Additionally, taking hard CC transforms you into an invincible deer, wasting the enemy's CC skills, granting very high survivability and making you excel at continuously providing vision and disruption.
hero_085.weaknesses: Individual burst damage and durability are very low, and you cannot utilize your strengths without allies around. Also, the passive deer transformation only triggers once every 60 seconds, so if you take hard CC while it's on cooldown, there's a high risk of being killed outright. You must always be careful with your positioning.

hero_086.earlyGame: Utilize your passive, which allows you to ignore terrain while flying, to quickly clear the jungle and aim for ganks. Since your basic attacks deal percentage damage, it's easy to deal high burst damage from the early game. Use Skill 1's dash to get behind the enemy, stun them to secure a clear advantage, and take control of the match.
hero_086.midGame: By fighting while activating Skill 2 to reduce incoming damage by 30%, forced engagements become possible. To maintain the flying state, make good use of the terrain and continuously deal damage to the enemy to maintain movement speed and armor penetration buffs. This is the key to improving your mid-game combat capability.
hero_086.lateGame: In the late game, enemy damage increases, so defenseless charges are dangerous. Utilize the 0.5-second untargetable state from Skill 3's leap to dodge fatal enemy skills, and play the role of disrupting the enemy formation with knock-ups and rain of blades from your dive. Chipping away at the enemy frontline with percentage damage is also effective.
hero_086.teamfight: Approach from outside the enemy's vision by ignoring terrain in the flying state, and use Skill 1 to dash into the enemy backline and stun them. Respond to enemy counterattacks with the untargetable state of Skill 3, and while dealing AoE knock-ups and continuous damage, utilize the passive percentage damage to disrupt the enemy ranks.
hero_086.commonMistakes: If the flying state is canceled, the percentage damage of melee attacks and the ability to ignore terrain are lost, significantly reducing combat capability. It's a fatal mistake to let the flying state expire without dealing damage to enemies during combat. You must consciously maintain the flying state by constantly dealing damage or passing through terrain.
hero_086.strengths: The ability to completely ignore terrain allows for ganks and escapes from unpredictable routes. With passive percentage damage based on max HP, it's easy to deal damage even against tanks. The invincibility and AoE CC from Skill 3 give this hero extremely high potential as an assassin.
hero_086.weaknesses: When the flying state is canceled, mobility drops significantly, losing most of their strengths. Also, after dashing with Skill 1, you move behind the target, so if you misjudge your positioning, you risk being isolated in the middle of the enemy team and focused down. Judging the timing of entry is very strict.

hero_087.earlyGame: In the early game, fight in the initial mode, utilizing the dash of Skill 1 and the movement speed reduction and damage from the sword aura of Skill 2 to sustain in the lane. Hit enemy heroes with basic attacks and skills to gain EXP, level up quickly, and aim to switch to the powerful Domination Mode or Berserk Mode.
hero_087.midGame: Switch to Domination Mode with Skill 3 and gain maximum energy. In Domination Mode, physical damage reduction increases based on bonus attack speed, so manage your energy while fighting with high durability. Understand the energy acquisition mechanics when switching modes and use it appropriately.
hero_087.lateGame: In the late game, utilize the bonus physical attack and attack speed from items. Depending on the situation, choose either Berserk Mode for high attack speed or Domination Mode for high physical damage reduction. Grasp the energy consumption and acquisition mechanisms of each mode, and maintain the optimal form for your role in teamfights.
hero_087.teamfight: In Domination Mode, utilize high physical damage reduction to withstand damage on the frontline while poking enemies with Skill 2's AoE damage and movement speed reduction. In Berserk Mode, exert continuous damage with high attack speed, exploiting enemy openings. Switching forms according to the situation is crucial.
hero_087.commonMistakes: A common failure is neglecting energy management for the modes, running out of energy at critical moments, and failing to receive passive benefits (damage reduction or attack speed increase). Since energy is consumed every second, it's essential to manually switch modes at appropriate times before combat to replenish energy.
hero_087.strengths: The greatest strength is the ability to switch between two powerful forms: high damage reduction in Domination Mode and overwhelming attack speed in Berserk Mode. Although highly item-dependent, passive effects provide very high stats in both durability and damage output, allowing for flexible combat styles.
hero_087.weaknesses: The powerful passive effects of each mode rely on energy, which drains every second, carrying the risk of energy shortage in prolonged fights or unexpected engagements. Also, combat capability is extremely low in the initial mode, making the early game macro before leveling up a weak point.

hero_088.earlyGame: Deploy the longbow with Skill 1, increasing your range by 150 to safely poke enemies unilaterally. However, attack speed decreases and mana is consumed while the longbow is active, so secure an advantage by appropriately stowing the longbow to increase movement speed and recover mana with basic attacks for dynamic lane trades.
hero_088.midGame: Use the Magic-Piercing Arrow of Skill 2 to deal damage and a 0.5-second silence to enemies, aiming for further magic damage with the subsequent explosion. Because your passive allows you to deal extra damage to shields, aggressively attack enemies with shields to maximize your burst damage in mid-game skirmishes.
hero_088.lateGame: Once items are completed, deploy the magic circle of Skill 3 to gain critical hit rate and movement speed buffs. The passive, which applies a 30% movement speed reduction (up to 50% consecutively) upon landing critical hits, becomes extremely powerful, allowing you to unilaterally wipe out enemies from the ultra-long range of the longbow without letting them get close.
hero_088.teamfight: In teamfights, position yourself in the backline, deploy the magic circle with Skill 3, and boost the overall critical hit rate and movement speed of your allies. Attack from ultra-long range with the longbow, using the powerful slow effect from critical hits to root enemies. Enemies with shields are particularly vulnerable to your passive, so prioritize attacking them.
hero_088.commonMistakes: A typical mistake is maintaining the longbow state of Skill 1 for too long, exhausting your mana, and being unable to attack at crucial moments. Stowing the longbow recovers mana (triple the amount when attacking heroes), so it's essential to constantly monitor your remaining mana and consciously stow the bow between engagements to manage it.
hero_088.strengths: The strongest point is the ability to kite without allowing enemies to approach, thanks to the long range of Skill 1 and the movement speed reduction effect triggered by critical hits. Furthermore, the passive deals equal additional damage to enemy shields, allowing you to instantly neutralize enemy heroes reliant on shields.
hero_088.weaknesses: When the longbow is deployed, attack speed is reduced by 20%, and lacking any mobility skills, you are very squishy when approached by assassins. Also, mana consumption is intense, and aside from the movement speed buff from manually canceling Skill 3, you have no self-defense methods, meaning you are easily killed without allied protection.

hero_089.earlyGame: Utilize the passive enhanced basic attack (extra damage and HP recovery) that triggers every 6 seconds to gain an advantage in lane trades. Since there is also an Unyielding effect that increases physical defense every time you take damage, aggressively trade blows with enemies using the dash of Skill 1 and the sweep of Skill 2, applying pressure from the early game.
hero_089.midGame: Utilize the overwhelming mobility of the boat from Skill 3 to perform powerful ganks in other lanes. While sailing, you are in a super armor state, and crashing into an enemy deals explosive damage, knock-up, and grants 5 stacks of Unyielding. Use Skill 2 to further apply a slow and secure kills.
hero_089.lateGame: In the late game, utilize Skill 3 as a transport method for allied heroes. As the initiator of teamfights, charge into the enemy ranks with the boat, deliver a wide-area knock-up, then chain Skill 1's additional knock-up and Skill 2's continuous damage to lock down enemies, while enduring on the frontline with the physical defense increase from your passive.
hero_089.teamfight: The most powerful tactic is to put allies on the boat and launch a surprise attack on the enemy backline. Immediately after landing a wide-area knock-up with the boat crash, follow up with Skill 1 to chain another knock-up. Decrease the movement speed of all enemies with Skill 2 while gaining high defense from Unyielding stacks via incoming damage to hold the frontline.
hero_089.commonMistakes: The boat from Skill 3 explodes if it hits walls or towers, so stopping before hitting an enemy due to a steering error is fatal. Also, since Skill 2 can reset the basic attack cooldown each time it's cast, be aware that failing to weave basic attacks between Skill 2 casts will significantly drop your burst damage.
hero_089.strengths: The mobility to cover a wide area of the map with Skill 3, and the powerful hard CC (knock-up) generated from it, are the greatest weapons. High durability from physical defense increases and HP recovery via the passive, along with the super armor effect of the boat, make the ability to initiate with allies one of the best in the game.
hero_089.weaknesses: Steering Skill 3 requires practice, and hitting an obstacle results in a failed effect. Also, heavy reliance on skills means missing Skills 1 and 3 leaves you without mobility and CC, making you easily kited by enemies. Note that your defense is relatively low immediately after a gank if Unyielding stacks haven't built up.

hero_090.earlyGame: Combine Skills 1 and 2 to cross ink and brush strokes, triggering the passive AoE explosive damage to simultaneously clear minions and harass enemies. By touching the brush strokes, you gain HP recovery and an enhanced basic attack, so aim to reach level 4 while maintaining sustain in the lane.
hero_090.midGame: Once you obtain Skill 3, aggressively aim for kills. By overlapping the dash of Skill 3 towards the ink/brush strokes of Skills 1 and 2, you perform consecutive dashes, jumping into the air after succeeding 3 to 5 dashes. During the jump, you are untargetable and deal continuous magic damage, instantly assassinating squishy enemy heroes.
hero_090.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, you must perform Skill 3 dashes carefully to avoid enemy CC and focus fire. Dashes can be paused to weave in skills or attacks, so time it right, ensure 5 successful dashes, and unleash overwhelming AoE damage from an invincible state into the enemy formation.
hero_090.teamfight: Enter from the flank after allies initiate, prepare brush strokes with Skills 1 and 2, and then start dashing with Skill 3. Enter the invincible jump state to completely nullify enemy attacks while dealing massive damage with continuous strikes from the air. Since there is damage reduction against the same target, hitting multiple people is ideal.
hero_090.commonMistakes: Failing to hit enemies or brush strokes with Skill 3's dashes, missing the aerial jump, and being isolated in the enemy ranks is the mistake to avoid the most. Although the CD is partially reduced if the dash is interrupted, you won't become invincible and will be instantly killed, so practicing dashing along the paths of Skills 1 and 2 is mandatory.
hero_090.strengths: The overwhelming continuous magic damage from the 'untargetable' and 'all CC cleanse' state of Skill 3's jump is the greatest strength. Mobility is also extremely high, and the explosive damage and HP recovery from the passive combined with skills provide high-level capabilities in both single-target assassination and survivability.
hero_090.weaknesses: The operational difficulty of Skill 3 is extremely high; without successful dashes, decent burst damage or survivability cannot be demonstrated. Additionally, after the jump ends, you are completely defenseless, so if you fail to finish off the enemy, or receive hard CC during the dashes before becoming invincible, you will be helplessly taken down.

hero_091.earlyGame: In the early game, actively brawl utilizing the passive dual-sword attacks and healing. Hit enemies with basic attacks and skills to build up Rage, gaining an advantage with physical/magic penetration and damage reduction effects. Aim for knock-ups with Skill 1's upward slash, stack marks, and push forward with additional true damage and super armor.
hero_091.midGame: Utilize Skill 2's dash to close in on enemies, gaining movement speed and attack speed buffs for follow-up attacks. After acquiring Skill 3, target an enemy hero to approach in an untargetable state, applying immense pressure with physical/magic hybrid damage upon landing and mark acquisition. Flying to an ally to grant a shield and provide support is also effective.
hero_091.lateGame: Up to 40% armor penetration, 20% damage reduction, and 75% lifesteal from Rage become powerfully effective. Dive into the enemy ranks with Skill 3, smashing the ground to deal massive damage while building Rage. Utilize the true damage and super armor from Skill 1 at 3 stacks to slice through the enemy frontline.
hero_091.teamfight: Dive into the enemy's main force with Skill 3, or fly to a comrade in crisis to provide a shield while joining the fray. Due to passive Rage, you exhibit high durability and healing power even in the center of the enemy formation. Activate Skill 1 at max stacks to ignore enemy CC with super armor while delivering true damage.
hero_091.commonMistakes: Since durability is low without Rage, charging into the enemy formation without building it up means the passive reduction and healing won't take effect, resulting in being quickly killed. Also, since Skill 1's super armor and true damage don't trigger without 3 stacks of marks, wasting the skill by neglecting stack management is dangerous.
hero_091.strengths: Being able to wield physical, magic, and true damage makes this hero highly effective against enemy defensive items, which is their greatest strength. Along with penetration, damage reduction, and high lifesteal from Rage, Skill 3's untargetability and ally shielding boast unrivaled tenacity and burst damage in both 1v1s and teamfights.
hero_091.weaknesses: Because you need to build Rage and marks, true potential cannot be demonstrated unless you get close and consistently land attacks. If continuously kited, stacks cannot be maintained, lowering both durability and burst damage. Additionally, since the special effects of basic attacks only trigger at 60%, reliance on equipment needs attention.

hero_092.earlyGame: Dominate lanes and the jungle utilizing the continuous damage of the passive Soul Chain and the Path of Rebirth of Skill 1. On the path, you gain the ability to ignore terrain and increased movement speed, so use walls to accelerate the path and aim for ganks. Use the execution effect of the Divine Shaman mode upon reactivation to securely clear monsters and secure kills in the early game.
hero_092.midGame: Create the Boundary of Life and Death with Skill 2, applying marks to enemies it touches. Sustain the front line by gaining healing effects and extra damage from enhanced basic attacks against marked enemies. The Soul Chain can link up to 3 targets, so positioning to continuously deal damage and heal while involving multiple enemies is powerful.
hero_092.lateGame: Unleash the power of the Divine Shaman with Skill 3, diving from the sky to deal physical damage and knockback to enemies. The more enemy heroes hit, the greater the healing effect, and you can summon spirits to fight alongside you. For 8 seconds after landing, you enter Divine Shaman mode, where basic attacks instantly kill enemies below 10% HP, making you the crux of teamfights.
hero_092.teamfight: Use the terrain ignorance of Skill 1 to enter from the optimal position, and dive into the enemy formation with Skill 3 to deal wide-area damage and knockback while recovering a massive amount of HP. Scatter marks with Skill 2, link with multiple targets via the Soul Chain to heal, and systematically eliminate weakened enemies with the execution effect of Divine Shaman mode.
hero_092.commonMistakes: The Soul Chain deals continuous damage by linking to enemies, so moving too far away and breaking the link significantly drops your burst damage. Also, since the healing and spirit effects of Skill 3 are weakened if it doesn't hit enemy heroes, wasting it in an empty area severely impairs your survivability and decisive power.
hero_092.strengths: Possesses excellent continuous combat capabilities: continuous healing from the passive Soul Chain and Skill 2, high mobility ignoring terrain with Skill 1, and HP percentage execution in Divine Shaman mode. The strength in chaotic fights is overwhelming, utilizing the numerical advantage of summoned spirits and simultaneously attacking/healing multiple enemies.
hero_092.weaknesses: Burst damage is relatively low, relying heavily on the continuous damage of the Soul Chain, so if enemies escape quickly, full damage cannot be dealt. Additionally, you need to stay near the enemy formation to leverage healing and execution effects, meaning if you get locked down by a powerful CC chain, you will be taken down without doing anything.

hero_093.earlyGame: Understand the passive that saves elements by casting skills twice, and manage your lane. Choose between Fire (Skill 1) for extra and true damage, or Water (Skill 2) for HP recovery, depending on the situation. Build Dragon Soul marks by hitting skills, and engage in favorable damage trades with enhanced basic attacks.
hero_093.midGame: Since effects are amplified by combining main and sub elements, skill rotation tailored to the combat situation is vital. When pursuing, use Wind (Skill 3) to increase movement speed and apply knockbacks; when durability is needed, stack Water to increase healing by 50%, sustaining mid-game fights with powerful basic attacks consuming marks.
hero_093.lateGame: In the late game, stack Fire to maximize true damage and extra damage, unleashing burst damage that crushes even enemy tanks. Elements update with every skill use, so advanced mechanics are required to constantly track which skills were used last two, consuming up to 3 stacks of Dragon Soul marks to deliver consecutive attacks.
hero_093.teamfight: Flexibly switch elements based on the situation. If you want to lock down enemies, focus on Wind for consecutive knockbacks; to deal burst damage, charge in with Fire as the main element. Utilize self-healing from Skill 2 and AoE splash damage from skills to fight on the frontline, catching many enemies in the crossfire.
hero_093.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is ignoring the passive 'last two skill elements' and indiscriminately spamming skills, triggering unintended enhanced basic attacks. Getting Water or Wind effects when you need to output damage lets enemies escape, so you should make it a habit to constantly check the icons and status of your current main and sub elements.
hero_093.strengths: The versatility to handle any situation by selectively using the three elements of Fire (burst/true damage), Water (healing/durability), and Wind (mobility/CC) is the greatest strength. Furthermore, with AoE damage from skills and consecutive attacks upon consuming marks, this hero boasts high combat power in both 1v1s and teamfights.
hero_093.weaknesses: Because effects change drastically depending on the order of skill usage, the difficulty of operation and situational judgment is extremely high. Failing to manage the passive elements often results in being a jack-of-all-trades, master of none. Lacking defensive stat boosts, you expose your squishiness if you receive focus fire or CC that outpaces the healing of the Water element.

hero_094.earlyGame: Be conscious of your passive Moon Ring, fighting while applying marks to enemies with the Moonlight of Skill 1 and the Orbs of Skill 2. Enhanced basic attacks after fully consuming the Moon Ring generate Orbs that deal extra magic damage, so skillfully interweave skills and basic attacks from the early game to take the initiative in the lane.
hero_094.midGame: Utilize the additional effects against marked enemies. Landing Skill 1 for true damage and Skill 2 for a stun creates a powerful combo. In dangerous situations, use the link from Skill 3 to gain damage reduction, enter the Water Moon Illusion to gain a shield, reduce skill cooldowns, and turn the tables to counterattack.
hero_094.lateGame: In the late game, the use of Skill 3 is the key. Link to the enemy carry to reduce incoming damage, protect yourself by entering the Illusion, and cast skills again within the Illusion to distance yourself from the enemy, bewildering them with unpredictable movements. It is also possible to initiate teamfights with true damage and stuns from marks.
hero_094.teamfight: Deploy Orbs with Skill 2 to deal damage to multiple enemies and generate shields, increasing mobility to position yourself. Link Skill 3 to the core hero of the enemy team to halve incoming damage, dragging them into the Illusion to create a favorable situation. Manage mark stacks to accurately deliver AoE stuns and true damage.
hero_094.commonMistakes: Neglecting Moon Ring stack management and failing to execute an enhanced basic attack at crucial moments is a heavy blow. Also, since the link from Skill 3 is canceled if you distance yourself too much, be extremely careful of the mistake where the enemy escapes and breaks the link before you enter the Illusion, denying you the shield and CD reduction benefits.
hero_094.strengths: Powerful additional effects of true damage and stuns against marked enemies, combined with high self-defense from 50% damage reduction and shielding via Skill 3, are the core strengths. Through cooldown reduction and distance manipulation via the Illusion, you can continuously scatter burst damage and CC while confounding enemies.
hero_094.weaknesses: To maximize burst damage and CC, the steps of 'applying marks' and 'consuming Moon Rings' are required, making instantaneous burst damage difficult to achieve. Also, the macro to maintain the link of Skill 3 is challenging, and if a highly mobile enemy breaks the link, you lose your defensive means and become squishy.

hero_095.earlyGame: Utilize the passive that recovers HP upon hitting skills to actively engage in damage trades during the laning phase. Be mindful of Star Power stacks, build the gauge with Skills 1 and 2, and continuously pressure enemies with the dash (enhanced basic attack) from enhanced skills at max stacks.
hero_095.midGame: Skill combos become diverse. Approach with the dash of Skill 2 to reduce movement speed, then continuously unleash enhanced skills. The passive Star Split, which converts 40% of incoming damage into damage over 2 seconds, provides more durability than meets the eye, allowing for aggressive plays even in small skirmishes.
hero_095.lateGame: The ability of Skill 3 to 'return to the position of 2 seconds ago' shines. After diving into the enemy ranks and wreaking havoc with Skills 1 and 2, return to a safe zone with Skill 3 while clearing Star Split damage and dealing massive damage to enemies on the path. Confound enemies with an endless combo interwoven with enhanced skills.
hero_095.teamfight: Always manage Star Power stacks and enter combat in an enhanced state. Cut into the enemy formation with Skill 2, chip away with the AoE damage of Skill 1, and just before enemy counterattacks or CC arrive, use Skill 3 to return to your original position. Play the role of disrupting the enemy formation with hit-and-away tactics, nullifying incoming damage while escaping.
hero_095.commonMistakes: Exhausting your skills without keeping track of Star Power stacks, leaving you stranded in the enemy formation unable to perform enhanced skills or enhanced basic attack dashes, is fatal. Also, if the 'position 2 seconds ago' returned to by Skill 3 is right in the middle of the enemy, you'll dive to your own death, so spatial awareness of past positions is essential.
hero_095.strengths: The overwhelming number of moves from spamming skills and the combo of enhanced skills/dashes via Star Power are the greatest strengths. With Star Split delaying incoming damage and Skill 3 rewinding positioning to cancel it out, you possess a tricky survivability that allows you to parry enemy attacks on the frontline despite being an assassin.
hero_095.weaknesses: The base damage of the skills isn't particularly high, so failing to execute combos accurately results in insufficient burst damage. Furthermore, since Skill 3's position rewinding is fixed at 2 seconds ago, the player's spatial awareness is tested, making the operational difficulty extremely high. Taking hard CC prevents skill rotation, leading to death from Star Split damage.

hero_096.earlyGame: Because the passive increases damage the further you are from the enemy, always maintain maximum range in combat. Place the energy spheres of Skill 2 on minion waves to safely clear the lane with continuous and explosive damage, while using the pull of Skill 1 to poke enemies and maintain a favorable distance.
hero_096.midGame: The tactic of applying 'Silk Binding Mark' to an enemy with Skill 1 and casting it again to pull the enemy towards your allies becomes powerful. Skill 2's energy spheres automatically track marked enemies, dealing massive damage simultaneously with the pull, picking off enemies one by one to secure kills in mid-game skirmishes.
hero_096.lateGame: Activate Skill 3 to empower yourself for 8 seconds and reset the CD of Skill 2. Moreover, the pull from the mark of Skill 1 can be used twice, allowing you to completely neutralize key enemy heroes. It is crucial to maximize the distance scaling passive to continuously provide CC and burst damage safely from the rear.
hero_096.teamfight: Position yourself at the very back of the team, activate Skill 3 to expand vision, and spam Skill 2 to poke. Don't miss naive enemy positioning; land Skill 1 to drag them into the allied formation twice, forcing isolation. When targeted yourself, use Skill 1 to push enemies away as a means of self-defense.
hero_096.commonMistakes: A common mistake is forgetting the mechanic that the pull effect won't trigger if you move too far from the enemy after applying the mark with Skill 1, missing CC opportunities by focusing solely on escaping. Exquisite positioning is required to maintain a safe distance (greater distance = more damage) while staying within the effective range of Skill 1.
hero_096.strengths: The unique and powerful hard CC of Skill 1, 'forcing the enemy's position to move in a specified direction', is the biggest strength. This allows you to drag the enemy carry in front of your allies or push them under towers. The CD reset and double movement effect from Skill 3 dramatically enhance this disruption capability.
hero_096.weaknesses: Self-defense relies entirely on the CC of Skill 1, and lacking any mobility skills makes you very weak when approached by assassins. Additionally, maintaining the distance necessary to leverage the passive is difficult, and missing the throw of Skill 1 significantly lowers your presence, making it hard to both deal burst damage and protect yourself.
